episode_text:
day=1; meal=strawberries with whipped cream; setting=Social with person in Front; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=open mouth; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit or another cold-served item AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social or personal THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed cold fruit meal was explicitly preferred without reheating. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person, TV, or device in front of the user AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred preserving the front view in a front-facing social context. No other directional variation observed yet.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition such as fruit pieces AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-acquisition-success option in the observed feeding episode. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any fork-fed food THEN yes BECAUSE the user accepted explicit web confirmation of bite acquisition, consistent with a preference for reliability despite extra interaction time. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF food is being transferred for feeding AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward due to limited trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer less compatible with their physical abilities. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer AND affective_state is any AND dining context is any THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user selected inside-mouth transfer and their physical profile supports that mode. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with another person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user preferred a nonverbal readiness cue, which is compatible with reducing conversational disruption. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and still chose open-mouth initiation even in a social setting, indicating low-effort readiness signaling is acceptable despite possible conversational ambiguity.

IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for drinking in the observed social meal.

IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and selected open-mouth initiation for wiping in the observed social meal.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with another person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN LED BECAUSE the user preferred a nonverbal cue when the tool reached transfer position. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any context THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected the reliable low-effort completion method, which also fits their inability to reliably press buttons.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head-gesture, making automatic progression the most compatible completion method.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or head-gesture, making automatic progression the most compatible completion method.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a person or focal activity in front of the user AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user preferred reducing prolonged visual obstruction in a socially front-facing context.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user preferred limited dipping for strawberries with whipped cream in a social meal, consistent with reducing messiness. IF food has no dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is personal THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any assisted task THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a moderate wait that balances time efficiency with enough time to respond using assistive interaction methods. No context-dependent variation observed yet.
